verdicts.issue: Could not independently confirm pages 91-121 in 'Monetary Theory and Practice' (1984, Macmillan). Goodhart's Law is genuinely from this body of work; pages and exact chapter title plausible but not directly verified in 2 searches.

Author field 'DeepMind, Google' is reverse-formatted; corporate-author convention requires '{Google DeepMind}' (single brace-protected token) so BibTeX renders the publisher correctly. Per §4a, corporate authors are written as a single brace-protected name, not Last,First. Schema bug only; entry references the real Gemini blog post.

Schema corruption: @article entry contains both 'journal = {CoRR}' AND 'booktitle = {arXiv preprint arXiv:1706.02677}'. @article entries must not carry a booktitle field. Drop booktitle; the paper is the well-known Goyal et al. arXiv preprint (never appeared at a conference).

Graphcore Colossus MK2 IPU whitepaper PDF is hosted at Graphcore's marketing site; the URL has been stable but is a vendor PDF, not a peer-reviewed document. Author '{Graphcore}' is fine per corporate-author convention.

Title 'Deep Learning Model Compression (ii) by Ivy Gu Medium' contains the author byline ('by Ivy Gu') and source ('Medium') concatenated into the title. Title should be 'Deep Learning Model Compression (II)' only; author goes in author field; venue is Medium.

Author list ends with 'and others'; volume/number/pages all missing for an @article in IEEE TKDE. Could not confirm in IEEE Xplore that this exact paper exists with Gudivada/Rao as co-authors in TKDE 2017; ambiguous whether 'data quality considerations for big data and machine learning' is the IJAR paper (Vol 1) or a TKDE entry.
Note says 'Not cited in Vol.~1 chapters; retained for completeness'. Entry is a real arXiv preprint (1904.02296) by Guo & Liang on mobile payment security. Consider removing if truly uncited.

Duplicate of han2015deep (same paper: Han, Pool, Tran, Dally, 'Learning both Weights and Connections for Efficient Neural Networks', NeurIPS 2015). Han2015 entry has @article type with both journal=CoRR and booktitle=NeurIPS — schema-corrupt; han2015deep has the cleaner @inproceedings form. Recommend deleting Han2015 and reconciling all citations to han2015deep.

@article entry contains both 'journal = {arXiv preprint}' AND 'booktitle = {International Conference on Learning Representations}'. Should be a single venue: the paper is ICLR 2016 (Han, Mao, Dally, Deep Compression). Convert to @inproceedings, populate ICLR booktitle/publisher/year=2016.
@inproceedings entry has stray 'journal = {ISCA}' field alongside the correct booktitle. @inproceedings should not carry journal; drop it. Otherwise venue/DOI/pages are correct.

Author 'School, Harvard Law' is Last,First-formatted on a corporate name; renders as 'Harvard Law School' is fine, but BibTeX will treat 'School' as a surname. Use single brace-protected '{Harvard Law School}' per §4a corporate-author convention.

@inproceedings has stray 'journal = {Proceedings of the IEEE conference on computer vision and pattern recognition}' alongside the correct booktitle. Drop journal field; @inproceedings should only have booktitle.

@incollection has stray 'journal = {European Conference on Computer Vision}' alongside the correct booktitle 'Computer Vision – ECCV 2018'. Drop journal field. Otherwise verified (AMC paper, ECCV 2018, He, Lin, Liu, Wang, Li, Han).

Has both journal (AAAI Proceedings) and booktitle (AAAI 32nd conference). For AAAI proceedings entries, @inproceedings is conventional, but the AAAI Proceedings is also indexed as a journal in Crossref so the dual fields are tolerated. Not flagged broken.

Cross-key/content mismatch: 'journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2007.03051}' but the DOI/url/eprint all point to arXiv:2005.04305. Title 'Measuring the Algorithmic Efficiency of Neural Networks' matches arXiv:2005.04305 (Hernandez & Brown, May 2020). The 2007.03051 reference in the journal field is wrong.

DOI 10.1002/0471743984.vse0673 points to a Wiley reference-work chapter (encyclopedia entry) unrelated to the Hinton/Vinyals/Dean knowledge-distillation paper. The arXiv id (1503.02531) and title are correct; the DOI and isbn fields are spurious silent corruption that will mislead readers and break the URL. Strip the DOI/isbn/publisher (Wiley) and keep this as a NeurIPS 2015 Deep Learning Workshop paper / arXiv preprint.
Has duplicate journal/booktitle both = 'Science' — schema redundancy but not corruption. Could drop booktitle for cleanliness.

Duplicate concept with hooker2021hardware (preprint vs CACM publication of the same paper). Both appear legitimate as separate refs but consider consolidating to hooker2021hardware (CACM has DOI/pages).

@article entry has both 'journal = {ArXiv preprint}' and 'booktitle = {arXiv preprint arXiv:1704.04861}'. Drop the booktitle; @article should not carry one. Otherwise correct (MobileNets v1 arXiv preprint).

Multiple silent-corruption defects: (1) Authors are wrong: actual paper is by Ting-Kuei Hu, Tianlong Chen, Haotao Wang, Zhangyang Wang (not Hu, Bowen and Zhang, Zhiqiang and Fu, Yun). (2) Year is wrong: paper is ICLR 2020, not NeurIPS 2021. (3) Venue is wrong: ICLR 2020 (OpenReview), not NeurIPS Vol 34. (4) Pages 18537-18550 do not match (those are a different NeurIPS 34 paper). The arXiv id is 2002.10025. The key first-name 'Hu' and topic 'triple' resolve, but everything else is corrupted; per §5 this is the silent-corruption class. Consider rename to hu2020triple.

suggested_fix.author: Hu, Ting-Kuei and Chen, Tianlong and Wang, Haotao and Wang, Zhangyang
suggested_fix.note: Rename requires propagating [@hu2021triple] -> [@hu2020triple] across all .qmd citations in vol1/vol2.

verdicts.issue: Silent-corruption class (per bib-check §5): key prefix is 'Huang' but author field is Kwon, Lee, Rhu (the paper is Kwon, Youngeun and Rhu, Minsoo, with Lee added incorrectly). Year in key is 2019 but the paper was presented at MICRO-51 in October 2018 (off by 1 — within tolerance for the year check, but the surname token is fully wrong). The conference name 'MICRO 52nd' is also wrong; the paper is at MICRO-51 (2018). Citations rendering as '(Kwon et al., 2019)' under [@Huang2019] is exactly the failure pattern flagged in the rule. Note: 'Lee, Yunjae' appears not to be a co-author of the original 2018 MICRO paper (the 2018 paper is Kwon & Rhu only, 2-author). DOI 10.1145/3352460.3358284 verified.

suggested_fix.author: Kwon, Youngeun and Rhu, Minsoo
suggested_fix.booktitle: Proceedings of the 51st Annual IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Microarchitecture (MICRO)
suggested_fix.note: Rename requires propagating [@Huang2019] -> [@kwon2018memory] across all .qmd citations. Verify whether the chapter actually intended this paper or some other Huang 2019 work.

verdicts.issue: Author 'Corporation, Intel' is Last,First-formatted on a corporate name; BibTeX will treat 'Corporation' as a surname and render incorrectly (e.g., 'I. Corporation'). Per §4a use single brace-protected '{Intel Corporation}'. URL is the AMX programming reference landing page which is also stable.
